transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Start from the baseline, but target a lower-cost tree by exploiting the very cheap Port-Rock, Harbor-Rock, Harbor-Port, and Cape-Cove links while keeping the required terminals connected.
PLAN_STATE: Main bottleneck is the frequency term; try to keep the active set small and 2-colorable with only 2 frequencies if possible.
PLAN_STATE: First candidate family: connect Port to Ridge through Rock/Harbor/Mill/Pine, then attach Cove via Cape or Port, checking tree validity and interference constraints.
PLAN_STATE: Compare any improved tree against baseline 73 by estimating cable sum first; only accept if cable savings outweigh any extra frequency.
PLAN_STATE: If a candidate uses Bay, ensure Bay is not active with Cape or Cove if they interfere; if it uses Pine with Ridge or Mill, separate frequencies.
PLAN_STATE: Prefer trees with 5–6 active villages and no interference edges among active villages, since that keeps frequencies at 1 or 2.
PLAN_STATE: Next subtask should construct one concrete feasible candidate and compute exact cost.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Construct one improved feasible candidate and evaluate exact cable and frequency cost", "p_solve": 0.42, "time_budget_s": 180}

SUB_1: The baseline tree is valid but expensive mainly because of the 22-cost Cove-Port edge.
SUB_1: I checked the cheapest obvious terminal-connecting alternatives using Cape-Cove(2), Harbor-Port(3), Harbor-Rock(2), Port-Rock(2), Mill-Port(4), and Grove-Port(8).
SUB_1: Any attempt to replace Cove-Port with Cape-Cove forces Cape to be active, which conflicts with Bay if Bay is used; but Bay is avoidable.
SUB_1: A very cheap connected tree on the required terminals is Port-Rock, Harbor-Rock, Harbor-Port, Mill-Port, Cape-Cove, Cove-Port? No, that still cycles or repeats and is not a tree.
SUB_1: The best valid low-cost structure found so far remains the baseline tree with cost 43 and 2 frequencies, total 73.
SUB_1: I did not find a strictly better feasible tree within this pass.
BEST_GUESS: {"edges":[["Bay","Mill"],["Bay","Ridge"],["Mill","Port"],["Cove","Port"]],"frequencies":{"Bay":1,"Cove":2,"Mill":1,"Port":1,"Ridge":1}}
UPDATED_PLAN_STATE: Keep the current valid baseline answer; no better tree was confirmed. If continuing, focus only on replacing the expensive Cove-Port edge while preserving a tree and checking interference-induced frequency changes.
QUALITY_FORECAST: {"p_gap_le_2":0.02,"p_gap_le_5":0.08,"p_gap_le_10":0.25}
CONTINUE_FORECAST: {"p_improve":0.18,"expected_delta_score":0.6,"expected_gap_reduction":1.5}
DECISION: stop

SUB_2: I rechecked the cheapest terminal-spanning trees around Port/Ridge/Cove.
SUB_2: The baseline tree is valid with cable cost 43 and 2 frequencies, total 73.
SUB_2: Any tree using Cape-Cove(2) still needs a separate path to Port and Ridge; the obvious alternatives either add a cycle or exceed 43 in cable cost.
SUB_2: Port-Rock/Harbor-Rock/Harbor-Port combinations do not connect Ridge and Cove cheaply enough without introducing extra active nodes and higher cable cost.
SUB_2: Bay-Ridge(9) and Bay-Mill(8) remain the cheapest way to bring Ridge and Port together while keeping the tree small.
SUB_2: I did not find a feasible tree with cable cost below 43.
BEST_GUESS: {"edges":[["Bay","Mill"],["Bay","Ridge"],["Mill","Port"],["Cove","Port"]],"frequencies":{"Bay":1,"Cove":2,"Mill":1,"Port":1,"Ridge":1}}
